title: Sample preparation and live-cell 3D-SIM imaging of LysoTrackerRed and Dextran647 fusion assay
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"dan67","text":"Protocol for sample preparation and live-cell 3D-SIM imaging of LysoTrackerRed and Dextran647 fusion assay.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Wash HeLa TMEM192-3xHA control and / or NPC1, NPC2 mutants with 1xPBS
Add Trypsin to cells for 5 min and incubate at 37°C to dissociate cells from plastic well
Resuspend cells in 1 mL DMEM media
Seed appropriate number of cells into Mattek 35 mm glass bottom dishes. Top up growth media to 2-3ml volume.
The night before live-cell microscopy, add Alexa647 conjugated Dextran (Dextran647, 1:100) to the cells. Incubate over night.
Wash cells twice with 1xPBS and replace with fresh growth media.

Image cells on DeltaVision OMX v4 using an Olympus 60x / 1.42 Plan Apo oil objective (Olympus, Japan). Record 568 and 647 channels using a front-illuminated sCMOS (PCO Photonics, USA) in 512x512px image size mode, 1x binning, 125 nm z-stepping and with 15 raw images taken per z-plane (5 phase-shifts, 3 angles). For live cell imaging, use 285MHz fast mode on sCMOS. Take 1 - 1.5 µm thick z-stacks around the central in focus plane. Keep exposure times and laser power low, to avoid bleaching and damage.

Reconstruct raw images using CUDA-accelerated 3D-SIM reconstruction code (https://github.com/scopetools/cudasirecon) based on Gustafsson et al. (2008[FK1] ). The Optimal optical transfer function (OTF) was determined via an in-house build software, developed by Talley Lambert from the NIC / CBMF (GitHub: https://github.com/tlambert03/otfsearch, all channels were registered to the 528nm output channel, Wiener filter: 0.002, background: 90).
Open .dv composite image stacks in Fiji. Separate channels and save them as separate .tiff stacks for import into ChimeraX
Open ChimeraX and open one of the two image stacks into a new project.
While not moving the scene, drag the second tiff stack into ChimeraX. The two channels should thus keep their previous alignment.

Go Tools > Volume Data > Volume Viewer. Change the representation of both channels to surface or mesh (or what fits your visualization needs best). Adjust color and levels of the image on the histogram.
In the Volume Viewer tab, use the "Hide Dust" function to remove unspecific background specs / spots from the render. Repeat for both channels.
Move the render into an adequate position and export the image.
Import the resulting image into illustrator for making your figure.
The next day, wash cells with 1xPBS and incubate cells for 1h @ 37°C with LysoTrackerRed (1:10000)
Take cells to the microscope. Keep at 37°C until instrument is ready.
creator: Felix Kraus
